skill.name: writing-tests
skill.description: Guidelines for writing short, maintainable backend tests with reusable fixtures and setup helpers.
skill.summary: Helps teams write concise backend tests with reusable setup patterns.
skill.author: Crossbill-Highlights

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak cryptography at SKILL.md line 3 and system reconnaissance at SKILL.md line 7. Both findings are false positives in plain backend testing guidance, with no commands, data access, network access, or prompt-injection language found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The static finding at SKILL.md line 3 appears to match text in front matter, not cryptographic code. The line is a skill description about backend test guidelines and does not mention or implement weak algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced line is YAML description text, not executable code. No evidence of cryptographic operations was found in the reviewed skill file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The static finding at SKILL.md line 7 appears in guidance about using fixtures and setup functions for tests. It contains no host inspection, process listing, environment probing, or system information collection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced line is plain testing advice about avoiding repeated setup steps. No reconnaissance behavior or command execution is present.

content.value_statement: Backend tests can become hard to maintain when setup logic is repeated across many cases. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to keep tests short and reuse fixtures.
actual_capabilities: Recommends short individual backend test cases.
Encourages shared fixtures for common test data.
Guides use of setup helpers when tests need preparation.
Helps reduce repeated setup steps across multiple tests.
Focuses test writing on maintainability and coverage.
limitations: Does not provide framework-specific syntax or examples.
Does not generate runnable test files by itself.
Does not cover frontend, browser, or end-to-end testing.
Does not define assertions, mocking rules, or test naming standards.

use_cases.title: Clean up backend unit tests
use_cases.description: Refactor repetitive setup into fixtures and helpers while keeping each test focused.

use_cases.title: Review test maintainability
use_cases.description: Check a backend test plan for repeated setup and overly long test cases.

use_cases.title: Guide junior contributors
use_cases.description: Give simple testing rules before a contributor adds backend coverage.

prompt_templates.title: Improve a simple test
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this backend test idea. Make the test case shorter and suggest fixtures for common data.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner use when drafting a first backend test.

prompt_templates.title: Plan reusable setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Given these backend test cases, identify repeated setup steps and propose helper functions or fixtures.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate use when several tests share preparation logic.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor a test suite
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this backend test suite for maintainability. Suggest how to split long tests and reuse setup safely.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced use during test cleanup or code review.

prompt_templates.title: Create a coverage strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a backend testing strategy for this feature that keeps individual tests short and uses fixtures for common data.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning before implementing broader backend coverage.

output_examples.input: A test creates the same user, project, and permissions in five separate cases.
output_examples.output: Move the shared user, project, and permission setup into fixtures. Keep each test focused on one behavior and one expected result.

output_examples.input: A backend test includes setup, action, and several unrelated assertions.
output_examples.output: Split the test into smaller cases. Use a setup helper for preparation and keep each case tied to a single backend behavior.

output_examples.input: A reviewer asks whether a new backend test file is maintainable.
output_examples.output: Check for repeated setup, long test bodies, and missing fixtures. Recommend helpers only when they reduce repeated steps.

best_practices: Keep each backend test focused on one behavior.
Use fixtures for common test data shared across cases.
Use setup helpers when repeated preparation makes tests harder to read.
anti_patterns: Do not copy the same setup steps into many tests.
Do not combine several unrelated behaviors into one long test.
Do not hide important assertions inside overly broad helpers.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps write maintainable backend tests with short cases, fixtures, and reusable setup helpers.

faq.question: Does it choose a test framework?
faq.answer: No. It gives general backend testing guidance and does not require a specific framework.

faq.question: Can it generate complete test files?
faq.answer: It can guide test structure, but the skill itself only provides high-level testing rules.

faq.question: Is it useful for existing tests?
faq.answer: Yes. It can help review existing backend tests for repeated setup and long cases.

faq.question: Does it cover frontend testing?
faq.answer: No. The source guidance is limited to backend tests.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed skill contains plain testing guidance and no confirmed dangerous behavior.
